summary: Small single-story rectangular building with layered perimeter walls: a low outer rim of white blocks, a short foundation of wooden planks, a middle band of stonebrick and cobblestone accents with horizontal windows, a smooth white mid-band, a wooden plank top band and a stone slab parapet. Interior floor appears wooden. Roof is flat/roofless with raised slab border.

shape.overall_form: compact rectangular box with recessed central floor and raised slab perimeter
shape.symmetry: bilaterally symmetrical along the long axis (windows evenly spaced)

materials.name: wood
materials.role: upper and lower horizontal trim and interior floor (planks)

elements: row of horizontal windows (3-4 per visible long side) at chest-head height
white outer rim (low) surrounding building footprint
wood plank trim bands above and below the stone band
stone slab parapet around flat roof
rebuild_hints: Build a rectangular foundation of white blocks forming a low rim one block thick around the building footprint.
Place a bottom trim of oak (or wood) planks directly adjacent to the white rim, one block high.
Construct the main wall with a lower row of stonebrick (use stonebrick stairs/regular to match shown), include cobblestone at corners.
Insert a continuous horizontal band of windows (glass panes) separated by stonebrick columns; above that place a smooth white block band one block tall.
Add an upper band of wood planks one block tall, then cap walls with stone slab (stone slab parapet) to form a flat roof edge.
Floor the interior with wood planks; roof is flat/inset so leave center open or covered with wood as shown and surround with slabs.
uncertainties: Exact block types for the white band (could be quartz vs white concrete vs smooth quartz); identified generically as white blocks.
Whether the stone band uses full stonebrick blocks, stonebrick stairs, or a mix for detailing; assumed stonebrick.
Precise footprint dimensions: counted visually and estimated as 9x13; could be off by several blocks.
Possible additional detailing on unseen back side and presence/location of an entrance (not visible in provided views).
Interior layout and any internal walls or doors are not visible and therefore unknown.
